instruction: Investigate high CPU usage on Ubuntu server
response: Start with read-only diagnostics: 1) top -H -b -n1 | head -40 2) ps -eo pid,ppid,cmd,%mem,%cpu --sort=-%cpu | head. Then inspect recent changes in services and logs before restarting anything.

Assumption: this is Debian 12 with shell access. First run read-only checks: du -xhd1 / | sort -h and journalctl --disk-usage. Warning: rm -rf /var/log/* is high risk; confirm backups and retention policy before any deletion.
